days.title: Arrival in Paris
days.subtitle: Exploring the City of Light

timeline.title: Arrive at Paris Gare du Nord
timeline.detail: Your Eurostar train arrives at Gare du Nord. Head to the nearby metro station to get your day pass for local transport.

timeline.title: Check in to Hôtel de la Porte Dorée
timeline.detail: Drop off your luggage at this charming hotel located a 10-minute walk from the metro station. The hotel features vintage decor and a lovely garden.
timeline.mapQuery: Hôtel de la Porte Dorée, Paris, France

timeline.title: Lunch at Le Comptoir de la Gastronomie
timeline.detail: Savor a delicious French meal at this popular bistro. The duck confit and homemade pastries are highly recommended.
timeline.mapQuery: Le Comptoir de la Gastronomie, Paris, France

timeline.title: Visit the Louvre Museum
timeline.detail: Explore the world-renowned Louvre, home to thousands of artworks including the Mona Lisa. Be sure to book your tickets in advance to skip the lines.

timeline.title: Stroll through the Tuileries Garden
timeline.detail: Enjoy a leisurely walk through these beautiful gardens between the Louvre and Place de la Concorde. Perfect for photos and people-watching.

timeline.title: Dinner at Le Procope
timeline.detail: Dine at Paris's oldest restaurant, known for its historical ambiance and classic French dishes. The coq au vin is a must-try.

localTips: Buy a Paris Visite pass for unlimited metro travel — great value!
Louvre tickets often sell out; book online a few days before your visit.
Try to visit the Louvre on a weekday to avoid crowds.
Dining out in Paris often includes a couvert fee; check the menu for details.
Many museums are closed on Mondays; plan your visits accordingly.

days.title: Discovering Montmartre
days.subtitle: Artistic Charm and Views

timeline.title: Breakfast at Angelina
timeline.detail: Start your day with a famous hot chocolate and a croissant at this iconic tearoom. Make sure to try their rich Mont Blanc dessert.

timeline.title: Explore Montmartre
timeline.detail: Wander through the artistic streets of Montmartre, visiting sites like the Sacré-Cœur Basilica and Place du Tertre, where local artists display their work.

timeline.title: Lunch at Le Consulat
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional French lunch at this charming café with outdoor seating. Try their Quiche Lorraine for a true Parisian experience.

timeline.title: Visit the Musée d'Orsay
timeline.detail: Discover masterpieces from the Impressionist period at this iconic museum housed in a former railway station. Pre-purchase your tickets online to save time.

timeline.title: Photospot at Sacré-Cœur
timeline.detail: 📸 Photospot: Sacré-Cœur Basilica. Capture sweeping views of Paris from the steps of this beautiful basilica, particularly stunning at sunset.
timeline.tip: Best angle is from the steps facing the city; arrive early for the best light.

timeline.title: Dinner at Chez Janou
timeline.detail: Enjoy Provencal cuisine in a cozy bistro setting. The chocolate mousse here is a favorite among locals.

localTips: Purchase a museum pass for savings if touring multiple museums.
The best views of Paris are often from the top of Montmartre.
Consider taking a guided walking tour for deeper insights into the area’s history.

days.title: Iconic Parisian Landmarks
days.subtitle: Ending on a High Note

timeline.title: Breakfast at Café de Flore
timeline.detail: Enjoy a leisurely breakfast at this historic café, famous for its literary past. Be sure to try the omelettes and freshly baked pastries.

timeline.title: Visit the Eiffel Tower
timeline.detail: Take your time exploring the Eiffel Tower, either by climbing the stairs or taking the lift. Booking tickets in advance is a must to avoid long waits.

timeline.title: Lunch at La Fontaine de Mars
timeline.detail: Savor classic French dishes at this bistro near the Eiffel Tower, known for its duck dishes and charming ambiance.
timeline.mapQuery: La Fontaine de Mars, Paris, France

timeline.title: Seine River Cruise
timeline.detail: Relax on a scenic cruise along the Seine, seeing beautiful landmarks from a unique perspective. Ensure you choose a cruise that includes a guide for extra insights.

timeline.title: Final Photospot at Trocadéro Gardens
timeline.detail: 📸 Photospot: Trocadéro Gardens. Capture fantastic evening views of the Eiffel Tower illuminated at night — the perfect ending to your trip.
timeline.tip: Arrive just before sunset for the best light; use a wide-angle lens.

timeline.title: Dinner at Le Ciel de Paris
timeline.detail: Enjoy a farewell dinner at this rooftop restaurant with stunning skyline views. Their tasting menu is a fantastic way to experience French cuisine.
timeline.mapQuery: Le Ciel de Paris, Paris, France

localTips: Book Eiffel Tower tickets as early as possible; they sell out.
A Seine cruise is a great way to relax before your departure.
Consider dining at rooftop locations for spectacular views.

hotels.name: Hôtel de la Porte Dorée
hotels.note: 10-min walk from the metro with vintage charm.

budget.note: Advance ticket from London to Paris.

budget.note: Advance entry to the Eiffel Tower.
For cafes and street vendors across 3 days.

urgent.label: Book Musée d'Orsay tickets
urgent.note: Avoid the queues by booking ahead.
